judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports fully nail (a)-(c); Report 2 answers better overall by also recommending a global/env-level master gate, which Report 1 omits.

report_1.evidence:
Reminders **will** be sent. `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, line 11–12, decides it:
```python
if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
    return True
```

report_2.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. For a clinic that never touched the setting, `tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders")` returns `None`, and the resolver returns `True`. The deciding line is `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inside `auto_send_reminders_enabled`

report_1.evidence: No — the default is wrong. This is not a benign display preference; it auto-sends SMS/email to patients... I would change `feature_flags.py:12` to `return False` (default off / opt-in)

report_2.evidence: No, it is the wrong default, and I would change it... this flag triggers **outbound messaging to patients** (SMS + email)... The fix is at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-13`: invert the default so a missing/None value returns `False`

report_1.evidence: `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added directly beneath, and as an exact structural copy of, `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... For `compact_calendar_view` a default-on is harmless — it only picks a calendar layout... the two features have opposite risk profiles

report_2.evidence: `git show 25509a7` shows the file was created for SLOT-190 with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, whose `is None → return True` grandfathers existing tenants into a harmless UI preference... the reminder resolver was appended as a structurally identical clone of that function... the two flags are in different blast-radius categories

**Add an env-level master switch AND it with the per-tenant value** so the feature is globally no-op until deliberately turned on, giving a single kill switch. Currently there is no master gate — only the per-clinic value guards live sends.
